<think>I now have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile the inventory. I have enough to give a thorough feature inventory. Let me complete the task.

Let me organize my findings:

## 1. Top-level structure
- Root package `quickfix` with many .go files
- Subpackages: config, datadictionary, internal, log (composite, file, mongo, screen, sql), store (file, memory, mongo, sql), spec (FIX dictionaries), cmd (generate-fix), _sql (DB schemas: mssql, mysql, oracle, postgresql, sqlite3), _test, _test_data

## 2. Main package key files
- doc.go - package doc
- application.go - Application interface (OnCreate, OnLogon, OnLogout, ToAdmin, ToApp, FromAdmin, FromApp)
- acceptor.go - Acceptor
- initiator.go - Initiator
- dialer.go - dialer/proxy config
- session.go - core session
- session_state.go - state machine
- session_factory.go - builds sessions
- session_id.go, session_settings.go, settings.go - config/settings
- message.go, message_router.go, field_map.go, field.go, tag.go, tag_value.go - message model
- parser.go - FIX message parsing
- validation.go - message validation
- repeating_group.go - repeating groups
- registry.go - session registry
- connection.go - connection
- errors.go, session_rejects.go - error/reject handling
- tls.go - TLS config
- fix_*.go - typed field types (FIXBoolean, FIXInt, FIXFloat, FIXDecimal, FIXUDecimal, FIXString, FIXBytes, FIXUTCTimestamp)
- begin_string.go, msg_type.go - constants
- log.go, null_log.go - logging interface
- store.go, memory_store.go - store interface + memory store
- latent_state.go, not_session_time.go, logon_state.go, logout_state.go, in_session.go, resend_state.go, pending_timeout.go - states

## 3. Session states & transitions
States (sessionState interface): FixMsgIn, Timeout, IsLoggedOn, IsConnected, IsSessionTime, ShutdownNow, Stop
State hierarchy (embedded):
- inSessionTime (IsSessionTime true)
  - connected (IsConnected true)
    - connectedNotLoggedOn (IsLoggedOn false)
      - logonState
      - logoutState
    - loggedOn (IsLoggedOn true)
      - inSession
      - resendState
- latentState (not connected)
- notSessionTime
- pendingTimeout (wraps another state)

State machine: stateMachine with State, pendingStop, stopped, notifyOnInSessionTime
Transitions:
- Start → latentState, then CheckSessionTime
- Connect → logonState (initiator sends logon, sets logon timeout)
- Stop → pendingStop, state.Stop
- Disconnected → latentState (if was connected)
- Incoming → parse, fixMsgIn
- Timeout → state.Timeout
- CheckSessionTime → notSessionTime if out of range, latentState if back in range, session reset if crossed day boundary
- CheckResetTime → ResetSeqTime feature, sends logon with seqnum reset

State transitions detail:
- logonState.FixMsgIn → inSession (on success), latentState (on reject/timeout), handles targetTooHigh → doTargetTooHigh
- inSession handles logon, logout, resendRequest, sequenceReset, reject, testrequest, heartbeat; on logout → logoutState
- resendState → handles gap fills, chunked resend, message stash
- logoutState.Timeout → latentState
- pendingTimeout.Timeout (PeerTimeout) → latentState

## 4. Store implementations
MessageStore interface: NextSenderMsgSeqNum, NextTargetMsgSeqNum, Incr, Set, CreationTime, SetCreationTime, SaveMessage, SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum, GetMessages, IterateMessages, Refresh, Reset, Close
Implementations:
- memory (memory_store.go + store/memory)
- file (store/file)
- sql (store/sql)
- mongo (store/mongo)
Plus internal/testsuite/store_suite.go - shared test suite for store conformance

## 5. Config keys (comprehensive - all in config/configuration.go)
Session: BeginString, SenderCompID, SenderSubID, SenderLocationID, TargetCompID, TargetSubID, TargetLocationID, SessionQualifier, DefaultApplVerID, StartTime, EndTime, StartDay, EndDay, Weekdays, TimeZone, TimeStampPrecision, ResetOnLogon, RefreshOnLogon, ResetOnLogout, ResetOnDisconnect, ResetSeqTime
Validation: DataDictionary, TransportDataDictionary, AppDataDictionary, RejectInvalidMessage, AllowUnknownMessageFields, CheckUserDefinedFields, ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, ValidateFieldsHaveValues, CheckLatency, MaxLatency, InChanCapacity
Initiator: ReconnectInterval, LogoutTimeout, LogonTimeout, HeartBtInt, SocketConnectHost, SocketConnectPort, SocketTimeout, ProxyType, ProxyHost, ProxyPort, ProxyUser, ProxyPassword
Acceptor: SocketAcceptHost, SocketAcceptPort, HeartBtIntOverride, UseTCPProxy, DynamicSessions, DynamicQualifier
Security/TLS: SocketPrivateKeyFile, SocketCertificateFile, SocketCAFile, SocketPrivateKeyBytes, SocketCertificateBytes, SocketCABytes, SocketInsecureSkipVerify, SocketServerName, SocketMinimumTLSVersion, SocketUseSSL
Logging: FileLogPath, SQLLogDriver, SQLLogDataSourceName, SQLLogConnMaxLifetime, MongoLogConnection, MongoLogDatabase, MongoLogReplicaSet
Storage: PersistMessages, FileStorePath, FileStoreSync, SQLStoreDriver, SQLStoreDataSourceName, SQLStoreConnMaxLifetime, SQLStoreMessagesTableName, SQLStoreSessionsTableName, MongoStoreConnection, MongoStoreDatabase, MongoStoreReplicaSet
Misc: ResendRequestChunkSize, EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed, EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum

## 6. Dictionary support
datadictionary package: DataDictionary struct (FIXType, Major, Minor, ServicePack, FieldTypeByTag, FieldTypeByName, Messages, ComponentTypes, Header, Trailer)
- build.go, datadictionary.go, xml.go
- Supports: components, repeating groups, fields, message defs, header/trailer
- XML parsing
- spec/ has FIX40-FIX44, FIX50, FIX50SP1, FIX50SP2, FIXT11
- Validation: fixValidator (single dict) and fixtValidator (transport + app dict for FIXT1.1)
- AppDataDictionary supports per-ApplVerID dictionaries (prefix syntax AppDataDictionary.FIX.4.4)

## 7. Transport acceptor/initiator
Acceptor:
- Multiple listeners (map[string]net.Listener), per session host/port
- DynamicSessions / DynamicQualifier (sessions not preconfigured)
- ConnectionValidator (custom auth, mTLS, IP binding)
- NewListenerCallback (custom listener creation)
- SetTLSConfig, SetConnectionValidator, SetNewListenerCallback
- UseTCPProxy (HAProxy/ELB PROXY protocol via go-proxyproto)
- RemoteAddr lookup
- dynamicSessionsLoop

Initiator:
- Per-session goroutines, reconnect loop with ReconnectInterval
- TLS config per session
- loadDialerConfig - SOCKS proxy support (golang.org/x/net/proxy)
- SocketConnectHost/Port with failover alternates (SocketConnectPort<n>)
- waitForInSessionTime, waitForReconnectInterval
- SocketTimeout for TLS handshake

## 8. Features QuickFIX/Go has that QF/J might not (or notable)
- ResetSeqTime: time-based seq reset while connected (sends logon with reset) — relatively new feature
- InChanCapacity: bounded incoming message channel buffer
- SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum: atomic store op
- IterateMessages: streaming store iteration (vs GetMessages)
- DynamicSessions + DynamicQualifier for acceptors
- ConnectionValidator + NewListenerCallback hooks
- SOCKS proxy support for initiator (ProxyType=socks)
- TCP PROXY protocol support (HAProxy/ELB)
- SocketPrivateKeyBytes/CertificateBytes/CABytes: inline PEM bytes (not just files)
- Decimal types: FIXDecimal + FIXUDecimal (unsigned decimal) — uses shopspring/decimal likely
- FIXBytes type
- SQLStoreMessagesTableName / SQLStoreSessionsTableName configurable table names
- TimeStampPrecision up to NANOS (SECONDS/MILLIS/MICROS/NANOS)
- cmd/generate-fix: code generation tool
- Weekdays setting for daily sessions on specific days
- ResendRequestChunkSize
- EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum (tag 789), EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed (tag 369)

I should note these are candidates for parity comparison; some QF/J also has. I'll frame as "notable/candidate differences".

Let me write the final report now. I have enough evidence. I won't read more files given budget.</think>I now have a comprehensive picture of the QuickFIX/Go codebase. Let me compile the feature inventory.

# QuickFIX/Go Feature Inventory (`thrdpty/quickfix`)

A vendored copy of `github.com/quickfixgo/quickfix` (full-featured Go FIX engine). Note: all files are git-ignored (vendored), so I bypassed ignore filters with `find`/`ls`.

## 1. Top-level directory structure
- **Root package `quickfix`** (~80 `.go` files): core engine, states, message model, validation, acceptor/initiator.
- **`config/`** — config key constants (`configuration.go`).
- **`datadictionary/`** — XML dictionary parsing/model (`build.go`, `datadictionary.go`, `xml.go`).
- **`store/`** — message store backends: `file/`, `memory/`, `mongo/`, `sql/`.
- **`log/`** — log backends: `composite/`, `file/`, `mongo/`, `screen/`, `sql/`.
- **`internal/`** — `event.go`, `event_timer.go`, `time_range.go`, `session_settings.go`, `testsuite/store_suite.go` (shared store conformance suite).
- **`spec/`** — bundled dictionaries: FIX40–FIX44, FIX50, FIX50SP1, FIX50SP2, FIXT11.
- **`cmd/generate-fix`** — code-generation tool.
- **`_sql/`** — DB schemas for `mssql`, `mysql`, `oracle`, `postgresql`, `sqlite3` (with `embed.go`).
- **`_test/`, `_test_data/`**, `.devcontainer`, `.github`.

## 2. Main package — key files & responsibilities
- `application.go` — `Application` interface: `OnCreate`, `OnLogon`, `OnLogout`, `ToAdmin`, `ToApp`, `FromAdmin`, `FromApp`.
- `session.go` (24KB) — core session logic (send/recv, logon/logout handling, seq nums, gap fill/resend).
- `session_state.go` — the state machine (`stateMachine` + `sessionState` interface).
- `session_factory.go` — builds sessions from settings (maps config → session fields).
- `acceptor.go` / `initiator.go` / `dialer.go` / `connection.go` / `tls.go` — transport.
- `message.go`, `field_map.go`, `field.go`, `tag.go`, `tag_value.go`, `repeating_group.go`, `parser.go`, `msg_type.go`, `begin_string.go` — message model & parsing.
- `validation.go` — `Validator`/`ValidatorSettings` (fixValidator + fixtValidator).
- `message_router.go`, `registry.go` — routing + global session registry.
- `errors.go`, `session_rejects.go` — reject/error model.
- `fix_*.go` — typed field types: `FIXBoolean`, `FIXInt`, `FIXFloat`, `FIXDecimal`, `FIXUDecimal` (unsigned), `FIXString`, `FIXBytes`, `FIXUTCTimestamp`.
- `store.go`/`memory_store.go`, `log.go`/`null_log.go` — store & log interfaces.
- State files: `latent_state.go`, `not_session_time.go`, `logon_state.go`, `logout_state.go`, `in_session.go`, `resend_state.go`, `pending_timeout.go`.

## 3. Session states & transitions (`session_state.go`)
`sessionState` interface methods: `FixMsgIn`, `Timeout`, `IsLoggedOn`, `IsConnected`, `IsSessionTime`, `ShutdownNow`, `Stop`, `Stringer`.

Embedded trait hierarchy:
- `inSessionTime` → `connected` → `connectedNotLoggedOn` (→ `logonState`, `logoutState`); `connected` → `loggedOn` (→ `inSession`, `resendState`).
- Standalone: `latentState` (disconnected), `notSessionTime`, `pendingTimeout` (wraps another state).

`stateMachine` transitions: `Start`→`latentState`+`CheckSessionTime`; `Connect`→`logonState` (initiator sends logon, arms `LogonTimeout`); `Stop` sets `pendingStop`; `Disconnected`→`latentState`; `Incoming` parses via dictionary then `fixMsgIn`; `Timeout`→`state.Timeout`; `CheckSessionTime` → `notSessionTime` when out of range, `latentState` when back in range, and **session reset** when crossing a day boundary (`!IsInSameRange(CreationTime, now)`). `CheckResetTime` implements the **ResetSeqTime** feature (sends a logon-with-reset at a configured wall-clock time while staying connected).

Per-state flows: `logonState` handles `RejectLogon`/`targetTooLow`/`targetTooHigh`; `inSession` dispatches logon/logout/resendRequest/sequenceReset/reject/testRequest/heartbeat, →`logoutState` on logout; `resendState` does chunked resend + message stash + gap-fill handling; `logoutState.Timeout` & `pendingTimeout.Timeout(PeerTimeout)` → `latentState`.

## 4. Store implementations
`MessageStore` interface (`store.go`): `NextSender/TargetMsgSeqNum`, `Incr…`, `SetNext…`, `CreationTime`/`SetCreationTime`, `SaveMessage`, **`SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum`** (atomic), `GetMessages`, **`IterateMessages`** (streaming callback), `Refresh`, `Reset`, `Close`. Backends: **memory, file, sql, mongo**; `FileStoreSync` toggle; configurable SQL table names. Shared conformance suite in `internal/testsuite/store_suite.go`.

## 5. Config keys (`config/configuration.go`)
**Session:** BeginString, Sender/TargetCompID+SubID+LocationID, SessionQualifier, DefaultApplVerID, StartTime/EndTime, StartDay/EndDay, **Weekdays**, TimeZone, TimeStampPrecision (SECONDS/MILLIS/MICROS/NANOS), ResetOnLogon, RefreshOnLogon, ResetOnLogout, ResetOnDisconnect, **ResetSeqTime**.
**Validation:** DataDictionary, TransportDataDictionary, AppDataDictionary (supports per-ApplVerID prefix `AppDataDictionary.FIX.4.4`), RejectInvalidMessage, AllowUnknownMessageFields, CheckUserDefinedFields, ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, ValidateFieldsHaveValues, CheckLatency, MaxLatency, **InChanCapacity**.
**Initiator:** ReconnectInterval, LogoutTimeout, LogonTimeout, HeartBtInt, SocketConnectHost/Port (+ numbered failover alternates), SocketTimeout, **ProxyType/Host/Port/User/Password** (SOCKS).
**Acceptor:** SocketAcceptHost/Port, HeartBtIntOverride, **UseTCPProxy**, **DynamicSessions**, **DynamicQualifier**.
**Security/TLS:** SocketPrivateKeyFile/CertificateFile/CAFile, **SocketPrivateKeyBytes/CertificateBytes/CABytes** (inline PEM), SocketInsecureSkipVerify, SocketServerName (SNI), SocketMinimumTLSVersion, SocketUseSSL.
**Logging:** FileLogPath, SQLLog*, MongoLog*.
**Storage:** PersistMessages, FileStorePath, FileStoreSync, SQLStore* (incl. **SQLStoreMessagesTableName/SQLStoreSessionsTableName**), MongoStore*.
**Misc:** ResendRequestChunkSize, EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed (tag 369), EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum (tag 789).

## 6. Dictionary support
`datadictionary.DataDictionary` models FIXType/Major/Minor/ServicePack, `FieldTypeByTag/Name`, `Messages`, `ComponentTypes`, `Header`, `Trailer`. `MessagePart` = Field / RepeatingGroup / Component with required-flag tracking. XML parsing in `xml.go`/`build.go`. Validation split into `fixValidator` (single dict, FIX≤4.4) and `fixtValidator` (transport + app dict, FIXT1.1+). Bundled specs cover FIX4.0–5.0SP2 + FIXT1.1.

## 7. Transport: acceptor / initiator
**Acceptor** (`acceptor.go`): multi-listener (`map[string]net.Listener`), per-session host/port; **DynamicSessions**/DynamicQualifier (unconfigured peers); **`ConnectionValidator`** hook (custom auth/mTLS/IP binding); **`NewListenerCallback`** (custom listener creation); `SetTLSConfig`/`SetConnectionValidator`/`SetNewListenerCallback`; **UseTCPProxy** (HAProxy/ELB PROXY protocol via `go-proxyproto`); `RemoteAddr` lookup; `dynamicSessionsLoop`.
**Initiator** (`initiator.go`/`dialer.go`): per-session goroutines + reconnect loop using `ReconnectInterval`; per-session TLS; **SOCKS proxy** (`golang.org/x/net/proxy` via `loadDialerConfig`); numbered `SocketConnectHost/Port<n>` failover alternates; `SocketTimeout` for TLS handshake; `waitForInSessionTime`/`waitForReconnectInterval`.

## 8. Features QuickFIX/Go has that QF/J may lack (parity candidates)
These are notable/candidate differences worth verifying against QF/J:
- **`ResetSeqTime`** — scheduled wall-clock seq-num reset via logon while staying connected (`CheckResetTime`).
- **`InChanCapacity`** — bounded incoming-message channel buffer (backpressure).
- **`IterateMessages`** + **`SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum`** — streaming and atomic store ops.
- **`DynamicSessions`/`DynamicQualifier`** — acceptor admits unconfigured peers; disambiguates duplicate SenderCompIDs.
- **`ConnectionValidator`** & **`NewListenerCallback`** acceptor hooks (custom auth/mTLS/listener).
- **SOCKS proxy** support for initiators (`ProxyType=socks`).
- **TCP PROXY protocol** (`UseTCPProxy`) for HAProxy/ELB.
- **Inline PEM bytes** config (`SocketPrivateKeyBytes`/`CertificateBytes`/`CABytes`) — not just file paths.
- **Decimal field types** (`FIXDecimal`/`FIXUDecimal`) and `FIXBytes`.
- **Configurable SQL table names** (`SQLStoreMessagesTableName`/`SQLStoreSessionsTableName`).
- **NANOsecond timestamp precision** (`TimeStampPrecision=NANOS`).
- **`Weekdays`** setting for daily sessions restricted to specific weekdays.
- **`cmd/generate-fix`** codegen tool.

Standard features shared with QF/J (for the parity matrix): FIX 4.0–5.0SP2 + FIXT1.1; file/sql/mongo/memory stores; file/sql/mongo/screen/composite logs; repeating groups/components; FIXT transport/app dictionary split with per-ApplVerID app dictionaries; `ResendRequestChunkSize`; tags 369/789; reset-on-logon/logout/disconnect; refresh-on-logon (hot failover); session-time/week-long sessions; TLS/mTLS.
args.next_steps: Cross-check the §8 candidate list against QuickFIX/J's actual feature set (config keys, store/log interfaces, acceptor hooks) to confirm which are true gaps vs. parity. For the truefix port, prioritize matching: the session state machine & transitions, the MessageStore interface (especially IterateMessages/SaveMessageAndIncr), config key surface area, datadictionary model, and the acceptor/initiator extension hooks.

- **TCP PROXY protocol** (`UseTCPProxy`) for HAProxy/ELB.
- **Inline PEM bytes** config (`SocketPrivateKeyBytes`/`CertificateBytes`/`CABytes`) — not just file paths.
- **Decimal field types** (`FIXDecimal`/`FIXUDecimal`) and `FIXBytes`.
- **Configurable SQL table names** (`SQLStoreMessagesTableName`/`SQLStoreSessionsTableName`).
- **NANOsecond timestamp precision** (`TimeStampPrecision=NANOS`).
- **`Weekdays`** setting for daily sessions restricted to specific weekdays.
- **`cmd/generate-fix`** codegen tool.

Standard features shared with QF/J (for the parity matrix): FIX 4.0–5.0SP2 + FIXT1.1; file/sql/mongo/memory stores; file/sql/mongo/screen/composite logs; repeating groups/components; FIXT transport/app dictionary split with per-ApplVerID app dictionaries; `ResendRequestChunkSize`; tags 369/789; reset-on-logon/logout/disconnect; refresh-on-logon (hot failover); session-time/week-long sessions; TLS/mTLS.

Next steps:
Cross-check the §8 candidate list against QuickFIX/J's actual feature set (config keys, store/log interfaces, acceptor hooks) to confirm which are true gaps vs. parity. For the truefix port, prioritize matching: the session state machine & transitions, the MessageStore interface (especially IterateMessages/SaveMessageAndIncr), config key surface area, datadictionary model, and the acceptor/initiator extension hooks.

<think>I now have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile the inventory. I have enough to give a thorough feature inventory. Let me complete the task.

Let me organize my findings:

## 1. Top-level structure
- Root package `quickfix` with many .go files
- Subpackages: config, datadictionary, internal, log (composite, file, mongo, screen, sql), store (file, memory, mongo, sql), spec (FIX dictionaries), cmd (generate-fix), _sql (DB schemas: mssql, mysql, oracle, postgresql, sqlite3), _test, _test_data

## 2. Main package key files
- doc.go - package doc
- application.go - Application interface (OnCreate, OnLogon, OnLogout, ToAdmin, ToApp, FromAdmin, FromApp)
- acceptor.go - Acceptor
- initiator.go - Initiator
- dialer.go - dialer/proxy config
- session.go - core session
- session_state.go - state machine
- session_factory.go - builds sessions
- session_id.go, session_settings.go, settings.go - config/settings
- message.go, message_router.go, field_map.go, field.go, tag.go, tag_value.go - message model
- parser.go - FIX message parsing
- validation.go - message validation
- repeating_group.go - repeating groups
- registry.go - session registry
- connection.go - connection
- errors.go, session_rejects.go - error/reject handling
- tls.go - TLS config
- fix_*.go - typed field types (FIXBoolean, FIXInt, FIXFloat, FIXDecimal, FIXUDecimal, FIXString, FIXBytes, FIXUTCTimestamp)
- begin_string.go, msg_type.go - constants
- log.go, null_log.go - logging interface
- store.go, memory_store.go - store interface + memory store
- latent_state.go, not_session_time.go, logon_state.go, logout_state.go, in_session.go, resend_state.go, pending_timeout.go - states

## 3. Session states & transitions
States (sessionState interface): FixMsgIn, Timeout, IsLoggedOn, IsConnected, IsSessionTime, ShutdownNow, Stop
State hierarchy (embedded):
- inSessionTime (IsSessionTime true)
  - connected (IsConnected true)
    - connectedNotLoggedOn (IsLoggedOn false)
      - logonState
      - logoutState
    - loggedOn (IsLoggedOn true)
      - inSession
      - resendState
- latentState (not connected)
- notSessionTime
- pendingTimeout (wraps another state)

State machine: stateMachine with State, pendingStop, stopped, notifyOnInSessionTime
Transitions:
- Start → latentState, then CheckSessionTime
- Connect → logonState (initiator sends logon, sets logon timeout)
- Stop → pendingStop, state.Stop
- Disconnected → latentState (if was connected)
- Incoming → parse, fixMsgIn
- Timeout → state.Timeout
- CheckSessionTime → notSessionTime if out of range, latentState if back in range, session reset if crossed day boundary
- CheckResetTime → ResetSeqTime feature, sends logon with seqnum reset

State transitions detail:
- logonState.FixMsgIn → inSession (on success), latentState (on reject/timeout), handles targetTooHigh → doTargetTooHigh
- inSession handles logon, logout, resendRequest, sequenceReset, reject, testrequest, heartbeat; on logout → logoutState
- resendState → handles gap fills, chunked resend, message stash
- logoutState.Timeout → latentState
- pendingTimeout.Timeout (PeerTimeout) → latentState

## 4. Store implementations
MessageStore interface: NextSenderMsgSeqNum, NextTargetMsgSeqNum, Incr, Set, CreationTime, SetCreationTime, SaveMessage, SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum, GetMessages, IterateMessages, Refresh, Reset, Close
Implementations:
- memory (memory_store.go + store/memory)
- file (store/file)
- sql (store/sql)
- mongo (store/mongo)
Plus internal/testsuite/store_suite.go - shared test suite for store conformance

## 5. Config keys (comprehensive - all in config/configuration.go)
Session: BeginString, SenderCompID, SenderSubID, SenderLocationID, TargetCompID, TargetSubID, TargetLocationID, SessionQualifier, DefaultApplVerID, StartTime, EndTime, StartDay, EndDay, Weekdays, TimeZone, TimeStampPrecision, ResetOnLogon, RefreshOnLogon, ResetOnLogout, ResetOnDisconnect, ResetSeqTime
Validation: DataDictionary, TransportDataDictionary, AppDataDictionary, RejectInvalidMessage, AllowUnknownMessageFields, CheckUserDefinedFields, ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, ValidateFieldsHaveValues, CheckLatency, MaxLatency, InChanCapacity
Initiator: ReconnectInterval, LogoutTimeout, LogonTimeout, HeartBtInt, SocketConnectHost, SocketConnectPort, SocketTimeout, ProxyType, ProxyHost, ProxyPort, ProxyUser, ProxyPassword
Acceptor: SocketAcceptHost, SocketAcceptPort, HeartBtIntOverride, UseTCPProxy, DynamicSessions, DynamicQualifier
Security/TLS: SocketPrivateKeyFile, SocketCertificateFile, SocketCAFile, SocketPrivateKeyBytes, SocketCertificateBytes, SocketCABytes, SocketInsecureSkipVerify, SocketServerName, SocketMinimumTLSVersion, SocketUseSSL
Logging: FileLogPath, SQLLogDriver, SQLLogDataSourceName, SQLLogConnMaxLifetime, MongoLogConnection, MongoLogDatabase, MongoLogReplicaSet
Storage: PersistMessages, FileStorePath, FileStoreSync, SQLStoreDriver, SQLStoreDataSourceName, SQLStoreConnMaxLifetime, SQLStoreMessagesTableName, SQLStoreSessionsTableName, MongoStoreConnection, MongoStoreDatabase, MongoStoreReplicaSet
Misc: ResendRequestChunkSize, EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed, EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum

## 6. Dictionary support
datadictionary package: DataDictionary struct (FIXType, Major, Minor, ServicePack, FieldTypeByTag, FieldTypeByName, Messages, ComponentTypes, Header, Trailer)
- build.go, datadictionary.go, xml.go
- Supports: components, repeating groups, fields, message defs, header/trailer
- XML parsing
- spec/ has FIX40-FIX44, FIX50, FIX50SP1, FIX50SP2, FIXT11
- Validation: fixValidator (single dict) and fixtValidator (transport + app dict for FIXT1.1)
- AppDataDictionary supports per-ApplVerID dictionaries (prefix syntax AppDataDictionary.FIX.4.4)

## 7. Transport acceptor/initiator
Acceptor:
- Multiple listeners (map[string]net.Listener), per session host/port
- DynamicSessions / DynamicQualifier (sessions not preconfigured)
- ConnectionValidator (custom auth, mTLS, IP binding)
- NewListenerCallback (custom listener creation)
- SetTLSConfig, SetConnectionValidator, SetNewListenerCallback
- UseTCPProxy (HAProxy/ELB PROXY protocol via go-proxyproto)
- RemoteAddr lookup
- dynamicSessionsLoop

Initiator:
- Per-session goroutines, reconnect loop with ReconnectInterval
- TLS config per session
- loadDialerConfig - SOCKS proxy support (golang.org/x/net/proxy)
- SocketConnectHost/Port with failover alternates (SocketConnectPort<n>)
- waitForInSessionTime, waitForReconnectInterval
- SocketTimeout for TLS handshake

## 8. Features QuickFIX/Go has that QF/J might not (or notable)
- ResetSeqTime: time-based seq reset while connected (sends logon with reset) — relatively new feature
- InChanCapacity: bounded incoming message channel buffer
- SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum: atomic store op
- IterateMessages: streaming store iteration (vs GetMessages)
- DynamicSessions + DynamicQualifier for acceptors
- ConnectionValidator + NewListenerCallback hooks
- SOCKS proxy support for initiator (ProxyType=socks)
- TCP PROXY protocol support (HAProxy/ELB)
- SocketPrivateKeyBytes/CertificateBytes/CABytes: inline PEM bytes (not just files)
- Decimal types: FIXDecimal + FIXUDecimal (unsigned decimal) — uses shopspring/decimal likely
- FIXBytes type
- SQLStoreMessagesTableName / SQLStoreSessionsTableName configurable table names
- TimeStampPrecision up to NANOS (SECONDS/MILLIS/MICROS/NANOS)
- cmd/generate-fix: code generation tool
- Weekdays setting for daily sessions on specific days
- ResendRequestChunkSize
- EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum (tag 789), EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed (tag 369)

I should note these are candidates for parity comparison; some QF/J also has. I'll frame as "notable/candidate differences".

Let me write the final report now. I have enough evidence. I won't read more files given budget.</think>I now have a comprehensive picture of the QuickFIX/Go codebase. Let me compile the feature inventory.

args.result:
# QuickFIX/Go Feature Inventory (`thrdpty/quickfix`)

A vendored copy of `github.com/quickfixgo/quickfix` (full-featured Go FIX engine). Note: all files are git-ignored (vendored), so I bypassed ignore filters with `find`/`ls`.

## 1. Top-level directory structure
- **Root package `quickfix`** (~80 `.go` files): core engine, states, message model, validation, acceptor/initiator.
- **`config/`** — config key constants (`configuration.go`).
- **`datadictionary/`** — XML dictionary parsing/model (`build.go`, `datadictionary.go`, `xml.go`).
- **`store/`** — message store backends: `file/`, `memory/`, `mongo/`, `sql/`.
- **`log/`** — log backends: `composite/`, `file/`, `mongo/`, `screen/`, `sql/`.
- **`internal/`** — `event.go`, `event_timer.go`, `time_range.go`, `session_settings.go`, `testsuite/store_suite.go` (shared store conformance suite).
- **`spec/`** — bundled dictionaries: FIX40–FIX44, FIX50, FIX50SP1, FIX50SP2, FIXT11.
- **`cmd/generate-fix`** — code-generation tool.
- **`_sql/`** — DB schemas for `mssql`, `mysql`, `oracle`, `postgresql`, `sqlite3` (with `embed.go`).
- **`_test/`, `_test_data/`**, `.devcontainer`, `.github`.

## 2. Main package — key files & responsibilities
- `application.go` — `Application` interface: `OnCreate`, `OnLogon`, `OnLogout`, `ToAdmin`, `ToApp`, `FromAdmin`, `FromApp`.
- `session.go` (24KB) — core session logic (send/recv, logon/logout handling, seq nums, gap fill/resend).
- `session_state.go` — the state machine (`stateMachine` + `sessionState` interface).
- `session_factory.go` — builds sessions from settings (maps config → session fields).
- `acceptor.go` / `initiator.go` / `dialer.go` / `connection.go` / `tls.go` — transport.
- `message.go`, `field_map.go`, `field.go`, `tag.go`, `tag_value.go`, `repeating_group.go`, `parser.go`, `msg_type.go`, `begin_string.go` — message model & parsing.
- `validation.go` — `Validator`/`ValidatorSettings` (fixValidator + fixtValidator).
- `message_router.go`, `registry.go` — routing + global session registry.
- `errors.go`, `session_rejects.go` — reject/error model.
- `fix_*.go` — typed field types: `FIXBoolean`, `FIXInt`, `FIXFloat`, `FIXDecimal`, `FIXUDecimal` (unsigned), `FIXString`, `FIXBytes`, `FIXUTCTimestamp`.
- `store.go`/`memory_store.go`, `log.go`/`null_log.go` — store & log interfaces.
- State files: `latent_state.go`, `not_session_time.go`, `logon_state.go`, `logout_state.go`, `in_session.go`, `resend_state.go`, `pending_timeout.go`.

## 3. Session states & transitions (`session_state.go`)
`sessionState` interface methods: `FixMsgIn`, `Timeout`, `IsLoggedOn`, `IsConnected`, `IsSessionTime`, `ShutdownNow`, `Stop`, `Stringer`.

Embedded trait hierarchy:
- `inSessionTime` → `connected` → `connectedNotLoggedOn` (→ `logonState`, `logoutState`); `connected` → `loggedOn` (→ `inSession`, `resendState`).
- Standalone: `latentState` (disconnected), `notSessionTime`, `pendingTimeout` (wraps another state).

`stateMachine` transitions: `Start`→`latentState`+`CheckSessionTime`; `Connect`→`logonState` (initiator sends logon, arms `LogonTimeout`); `Stop` sets `pendingStop`; `Disconnected`→`latentState`; `Incoming` parses via dictionary then `fixMsgIn`; `Timeout`→`state.Timeout`; `CheckSessionTime` → `notSessionTime` when out of range, `latentState` when back in range, and **session reset** when crossing a day boundary (`!IsInSameRange(CreationTime, now)`). `CheckResetTime` implements the **ResetSeqTime** feature (sends a logon-with-reset at a configured wall-clock time while staying connected).

Per-state flows: `logonState` handles `RejectLogon`/`targetTooLow`/`targetTooHigh`; `inSession` dispatches logon/logout/resendRequest/sequenceReset/reject/testRequest/heartbeat, →`logoutState` on logout; `resendState` does chunked resend + message stash + gap-fill handling; `logoutState.Timeout` & `pendingTimeout.Timeout(PeerTimeout)` → `latentState`.

## 4. Store implementations
`MessageStore` interface (`store.go`): `NextSender/TargetMsgSeqNum`, `Incr…`, `SetNext…`, `CreationTime`/`SetCreationTime`, `SaveMessage`, **`SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum`** (atomic), `GetMessages`, **`IterateMessages`** (streaming callback), `Refresh`, `Reset`, `Close`. Backends: **memory, file, sql, mongo**; `FileStoreSync` toggle; configurable SQL table names. Shared conformance suite in `internal/testsuite/store_suite.go`.

## 5. Config keys (`config/configuration.go`)
**Session:** BeginString, Sender/TargetCompID+SubID+LocationID, SessionQualifier, DefaultApplVerID, StartTime/EndTime, StartDay/EndDay, **Weekdays**, TimeZone, TimeStampPrecision (SECONDS/MILLIS/MICROS/NANOS), ResetOnLogon, RefreshOnLogon, ResetOnLogout, ResetOnDisconnect, **ResetSeqTime**.
**Validation:** DataDictionary, TransportDataDictionary, AppDataDictionary (supports per-ApplVerID prefix `AppDataDictionary.FIX.4.4`), RejectInvalidMessage, AllowUnknownMessageFields, CheckUserDefinedFields, ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, ValidateFieldsHaveValues, CheckLatency, MaxLatency, **InChanCapacity**.
**Initiator:** ReconnectInterval, LogoutTimeout, LogonTimeout, HeartBtInt, SocketConnectHost/Port (+ numbered failover alternates), SocketTimeout, **ProxyType/Host/Port/User/Password** (SOCKS).
**Acceptor:** SocketAcceptHost/Port, HeartBtIntOverride, **UseTCPProxy**, **DynamicSessions**, **DynamicQualifier**.
**Security/TLS:** SocketPrivateKeyFile/CertificateFile/CAFile, **SocketPrivateKeyBytes/CertificateBytes/CABytes** (inline PEM), SocketInsecureSkipVerify, SocketServerName (SNI), SocketMinimumTLSVersion, SocketUseSSL.
**Logging:** FileLogPath, SQLLog*, MongoLog*.
**Storage:** PersistMessages, FileStorePath, FileStoreSync, SQLStore* (incl. **SQLStoreMessagesTableName/SQLStoreSessionsTableName**), MongoStore*.
**Misc:** ResendRequestChunkSize, EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed (tag 369), EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum (tag 789).

## 6. Dictionary support
`datadictionary.DataDictionary` models FIXType/Major/Minor/ServicePack, `FieldTypeByTag/Name`, `Messages`, `ComponentTypes`, `Header`, `Trailer`. `MessagePart` = Field / RepeatingGroup / Component with required-flag tracking. XML parsing in `xml.go`/`build.go`. Validation split into `fixValidator` (single dict, FIX≤4.4) and `fixtValidator` (transport + app dict, FIXT1.1+). Bundled specs cover FIX4.0–5.0SP2 + FIXT1.1.

## 7. Transport: acceptor / initiator
**Acceptor** (`acceptor.go`): multi-listener (`map[string]net.Listener`), per-session host/port; **DynamicSessions**/DynamicQualifier (unconfigured peers); **`ConnectionValidator`** hook (custom auth/mTLS/IP binding); **`NewListenerCallback`** (custom listener creation); `SetTLSConfig`/`SetConnectionValidator`/`SetNewListenerCallback`; **UseTCPProxy** (HAProxy/ELB PROXY protocol via `go-proxyproto`); `RemoteAddr` lookup; `dynamicSessionsLoop`.
**Initiator** (`initiator.go`/`dialer.go`): per-session goroutines + reconnect loop using `ReconnectInterval`; per-session TLS; **SOCKS proxy** (`golang.org/x/net/proxy` via `loadDialerConfig`); numbered `SocketConnectHost/Port<n>` failover alternates; `SocketTimeout` for TLS handshake; `waitForInSessionTime`/`waitForReconnectInterval`.

## 8. Features QuickFIX/Go has that QF/J may lack (parity candidates)
These are notable/candidate differences worth verifying against QF/J:
- **`ResetSeqTime`** — scheduled wall-clock seq-num reset via logon while staying connected (`CheckResetTime`).
- **`InChanCapacity`** — bounded incoming-message channel buffer (backpressure).
- **`IterateMessages`** + **`SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum`** — streaming and atomic store ops.
- **`DynamicSessions`/`DynamicQualifier`** — acceptor admits unconfigured peers; disambiguates duplicate SenderCompIDs.
- **`ConnectionValidator`** & **`NewListenerCallback`** acceptor hooks (custom auth/mTLS/listener).
- **SOCKS proxy** support for initiators (`ProxyType=socks`).
- **TCP PROXY protocol** (`UseTCPProxy`) for HAProxy/ELB.
- **Inline PEM bytes** config (`SocketPrivateKeyBytes`/`CertificateBytes`/`CABytes`) — not just file paths.
- **Decimal field types** (`FIXDecimal`/`FIXUDecimal`) and `FIXBytes`.
- **Configurable SQL table names** (`SQLStoreMessagesTableName`/`SQLStoreSessionsTableName`).
- **NANOsecond timestamp precision** (`TimeStampPrecision=NANOS`).
- **`Weekdays`** setting for daily sessions restricted to specific weekdays.
- **`cmd/generate-fix`** codegen tool.

Standard features shared with QF/J (for the parity matrix): FIX 4.0–5.0SP2 + FIXT1.1; file/sql/mongo/memory stores; file/sql/mongo/screen/composite logs; repeating groups/components; FIXT transport/app dictionary split with per-ApplVerID app dictionaries; `ResendRequestChunkSize`; tags 369/789; reset-on-logon/logout/disconnect; refresh-on-logon (hot failover); session-time/week-long sessions; TLS/mTLS.

For the truefix port, prioritize matching: the session state machine & transitions, the MessageStore interface (especially IterateMessages/SaveMessageAndIncr), config key surface area, datadictionary model, and the acceptor/initiator extension hooks.

functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when finding files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 1000). Use to limit output for broad patterns.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

file_filtering_options.description: Optional: Whether to respect ignore patterns from .gitignore or .geminiignore

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when listing files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

respect_gemini_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .geminiignore patterns when listing files. Defaults to true.

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), and PDF files. For images, videos or other multimedia files, use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool if available.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group.

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- This tool defaults to non-interactive execution. Set `interactive` to `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for ordinary one-shot commands such as `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact bash command to execute as `bash -c <command>`. Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

run_in_background.description: Set to true to run this command in the background. Returns immediately with task metadata and an output file path.

interactive.description: Request an attached interactive shell session. Use `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for normal one-shot commands like `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. Returns the lines containing matches, along with their file paths and line numbers.

pattern.description: The regular expression (regex) pattern to search for within file contents (e.g., 'function\s+myFunction', 'import\s+\{.*\}\s+from\s+.*').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

include.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 20, max: 150). Values above 150 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches.

functionDeclarations.name: complete_task
functionDeclarations.description:
Call this tool to submit your final result and complete the task. This is the ONLY way to finish.

This tool must be called ALONE in its turn. Do not call any other tools in the same turn. If you still need another tool, do not call this yet.

Your result MUST be an informative handoff for the caller (main agent), not a one-line summary.

Requirements for result:
- Use 2+ short paragraphs or a short markdown sectioned report
- Include what you did / found and the concrete evidence that supports it
- Include file paths, errors, command outcomes, or other artifacts when relevant
- Do NOT return only a title or a single-line summary
- Keep it concise, but detailed enough that the main agent can act without reopening your full trace
- Choose a status that matches the outcome: use 'success' for fully completed work, 'partial' for meaningful progress with straightforward continuation, 'blocked' for external blockers that you cannot resolve autonomously, and 'failure' for invalid or unrecoverable paths

## Findings

# QuickFIX/Go Feature Inventory (`thrdpty/quickfix`)

A vendored copy of `github.com/quickfixgo/quickfix` (full-featured Go FIX engine). Note: all files are git-ignored (vendored), so I bypassed ignore filters with `find`/`ls`.

## 1. Top-level directory structure
- **Root package `quickfix`** (~80 `.go` files): core engine, states, message model, validation, acceptor/initiator.
- **`config/`** — config key constants (`configuration.go`).
- **`datadictionary/`** — XML dictionary parsing/model (`build.go`, `datadictionary.go`, `xml.go`).
- **`store/`** — message store backends: `file/`, `memory/`, `mongo/`, `sql/`.
- **`log/`** — log backends: `composite/`, `file/`, `mongo/`, `screen/`, `sql/`.
- **`internal/`** — `event.go`, `event_timer.go`, `time_range.go`, `session_settings.go`, `testsuite/store_suite.go` (shared store conformance suite).
- **`spec/`** — bundled dictionaries: FIX40–FIX44, FIX50, FIX50SP1, FIX50SP2, FIXT11.
- **`cmd/generate-fix`** — code-generation tool.
- **`_sql/`** — DB schemas for `mssql`, `mysql`, `oracle`, `postgresql`, `sqlite3` (with `embed.go`).
- **`_test/`, `_test_data/`**, `.devcontainer`, `.github`.

## 2. Main package — key files & responsibilities
- `application.go` — `Application` interface: `OnCreate`, `OnLogon`, `OnLogout`, `ToAdmin`, `ToApp`, `FromAdmin`, `FromApp`.
- `session.go` (24KB) — core session logic (send/recv, logon/logout handling, seq nums, gap fill/resend).
- `session_state.go` — the state machine (`stateMachine` + `sessionState` interface).
- `session_factory.go` — builds sessions from settings (maps config → session fields).
- `acceptor.go` / `initiator.go` / `dialer.go` / `connection.go` / `tls.go` — transport.
- `message.go`, `field_map.go`, `field.go`, `tag.go`, `tag_value.go`, `repeating_group.go`, `parser.go`, `msg_type.go`, `begin_string.go` — message model & parsing.
- `validation.go` — `Validator`/`ValidatorSettings` (fixValidator + fixtValidator).
- `message_router.go`, `registry.go` — routing + global s
